title: Thermal Radiance Greenhouse
description: A glowing pot of coals radiates heat energy that feeds a translucent plant through custom shaders. The user manipulates coal intensity, wind direction, and spectral filters to grow the plant while conserving a fixed pool of thermal energy across multiple shader channels. Undo/redo lets the user backtrack through shader parameter states.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads PotOfCoals.glb and DiffuseTransmissionPlant.glb using GLTFLoader. Place the pot of coals at the center-left and the plant at the center-right on a dark ground plane. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at (0,2,5) with OrbitControls. Add a dim ambient light and a point light near the coals whose color and intensity are shader-driven.

Implement these custom shaders: (1) A coal emissive shader using ShaderMaterial that replaces the coal mesh materials, with uniforms for redChannel, greenChannel, and blueChannel intensity (0-1 each). The fragment shader should mix emissive glow colors based on these three channels. (2) A plant transmission shader using ShaderMaterial with uniforms for absorptionR, absorptionG, absorptionB and a growthFactor. The vertex shader should displace vertices outward by growthFactor to simulate growth. The fragment shader should simulate subsurface scattering tinted by the absorption values.

Conservation law: A fixed energy budget of 3.0 is split across the three color channels. Increasing one channel must proportionally decrease the others. The plant absorption uniforms are coupled to the coal channels so that energy emitted in red is absorbed as red growth, etc. The growthFactor is the weighted sum of all three absorption channels.

Create a UI panel with: three sliders for R/G/B coal intensity (coupled via conservation), a wind direction slider (0-360) that adds a directional bias to vertex displacement in the plant shader via a uniform, a spectral filter dropdown (Warm/Cool/Neutral) that applies a post-process tint via a custom ShaderPass on an EffectComposer, and Undo/Redo buttons that push and pop the full state (all slider values and filter selection) onto a history stack. The interaction requires 10-14 steps: adjusting each channel multiple times, changing wind, selecting filters, and using undo/redo to compare states. Display the remaining energy budget and current growth percentage as text overlays.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  redChannel: number,         // red intensity in coal emissive shader (0-1)
  greenChannel: number,         // green intensity in coal emissive shader (0-1)
  blueChannel: number,         // blue intensity in coal emissive shader (0-1)
  absorptionR: number,         // red absorption coefficient in plant shader (0-1)
  absorptionG: number,         // green absorption coefficient in plant shader (0-1)
  absorptionB: number,         // blue absorption coefficient in plant shader (0-1)
  growthFactor: number,         // plant vertex displacement magnitude (0-2)
  windDirectionX: number,         // wind force X component (-1 to 1)
  windDirectionZ: number,         // wind force Z component (-1 to 1)
  energyBudget: number,         // total fixed thermal energy across channels (always 3.0)
  undoStackSize: number,         // count of states in undo history
  canUndo: boolean,         // whether undo operation is available
}
